很好玩，打了两个档，第一个是玩的猎人，全程拿着鞭子加手枪打的，发现极度刮痧，花了10w盐搓了个凤凰之尾依旧刮痧，还记得人体树还是用牛鞭加凤凰盾硬打过去的，反正盾很好用，最后无非就是剑盾变成了鞭盾，甚至说凤凰之尾轻攻击是剑，重攻击才是鞭子。第二个档玩的大剑和重锤，体验极好，特别是打击感真没的说了，剪刀系列的大剑手感是真的好，特别是重攻击剪刀剪几下太解压了，反正就是很好玩吧，然后就是感觉流程还是有点短了，如果能多加一些地图和怪就好了。最后听说盐2有点不是很好，但是还是会勉强玩下的吧，如果大剑的手感还是一样好的话，那其他的就没事了。

Un muy buen Soulslike, se puede decir que es un Dark Souls (temática y filosofia) en 2D, bastante duro en muchos pasajes, una progresión lenta, pero satisfactoria y una variedad de armas, armaduras y estilo de combate muy surtido y con mucha profundidad ... y claro, obviamente no podía faltar un número insano de jefes para apaciguar.  Cazar sus logros es muy intuitivo, solo basta con explorar todo lo que veas, interactuar con todo lo que encuentres y tener buena memoria (es un laberinto colosal donde la ausencia de mapa se siente mucho) ... y claro, se aconseja darle una revisada a alguna guia previamente, si se quiere conseguir todo en una sola partida, porque hay un par de ellos fácilmente perdibles y que requieren salvar un guardado.

游戏是好游戏，但是没有地图，也没有指引。你看看同类游戏哪个没地图？一些区域是有明显pattern的倒还能认路。但是某些区域直接就是一大坨。  尤其是 Hager's Cavern 这个区域。这个区域包含游戏中一个又臭又长的关键路径：你必须在 Mire Of Stench 才能拿到开红墙的道具 --> Hager's Cavern 是去 Mire Of Stench 的唯一入口区域 --> 你需要拿到钥匙才能开门 --> 你需要打败区域boss才能拿到钥匙 --> 你需要开一个闸门才能进boss房 --> 你需要走一个非常隐蔽的路线才能找到这个闸门开关。在此之外，Hager's Cavern这个区域是典型的几乎不存在可记忆地图特征，这将导致你不停地迷路。并且这个区域还充斥着各种粪怪。  如果要把它跟黑魂三的法兰粪坑比较，法兰粪坑起码还能告诉你要点三个火，我知道要去三个塔，并且每个点火后会有显式的动画指引。而 Hager's Cavern 则满足了三无：一无指引，二无目标，三无特征。不看攻略你只能乱撞——哦对看了攻略你也只能乱撞因为两步就迷路了，纯纯吃了一大坨。

_Achievement Review:  Difficulty: 4/10 Approx. Time Invested: 25h (15h with save files) MP Achievements: No, but the game can be played entirely in co-op Misc. / Activities / Collectibles: No Missable Achievements: No, as long as you don't progress too far before cleaning up remaining achievements  In typical Souls-like fashion, I couldn't really tell you what the story was about. Did I care much about it? Not really. It also wasn't the first time I had started the game only to quit after a couple of hours. This time, however, things were different. Once the game finally clicked, I had a great time with it.  Salt & Sanctuary is a true Souls-like presented in a 2D Metroidvania style. It took me a little while to adjust to the format, but the level design was excellent and did a great job conveying the game's atmosphere.  _Gameplay:  After remapping the controls to a more traditional Souls-like setup and getting used to the enemy behavior and boss encounters, the experience became surprisingly smooth. At no point did I feel frustrated by either the level design or the gameplay loop.  The gameplay is straightforward: explore an area, fight your way through enemies, and defeat the area's boss. The combat mechanics are solid, and the variety of weapons and spells is impressive. Special mention goes to the scythe, which finally has a moveset that feels genuinely fun to use.  _Immersion:  Even though a 2D perspective can sometimes limit immersion and atmosphere, Salt & Sanctuary does a great job overcoming that challenge. Each region feels distinct, with its own visual identity, enemy types, and hidden secrets waiting to be discovered.  _Boss Design:  The bosses were one of the highlights of the game. Only one encounter felt a bit more gimmicky than I would have liked, but otherwise every boss was well designed, appropriately challenging, and equipped with a fair and varied moveset. None of the fights felt unfair. Learning attack patterns and making proper use of the dodge roll was usually enough to overcome any obstacle.  _Recommendation:  I would recommend Salt & Sanctuary to anyone who enjoys Souls-like games, especially those looking for a shorter experience that isn't overly difficult. As long as you pay attention to enemy patterns and mechanics, the game offers a satisfying challenge without becoming frustrating.  By the time the credits rolled, I was genuinely pleased with the experience. The achievement list was equally enjoyable to complete and felt rewarding from start to finish.  Rating: 7/10

This one of those games that you play, finish and shrug your shoulders at same time saying “well, that was ok”. Unfortunately Salt and Sanctuary borrows too much from Dark Souls to have its own identity outside of 2D souls-like copy, and what is innovative and tailor-made for this game is too shallow and far-stretched to be memorable and unique. Plus there are several, maybe not bugs, but design mistakes and oversight that make this game drift too close to average territory. And it’s really a pickle for me, because I don’t know how to write this review without sounding like a broken record.   Salt and Sanctuary is 2D metroidvania-lite, deeply rooted in a souls-like genre. In fact, there are more souls-like than metroidvania here, so much that if you ever finished any original Dark Souls game, you will feel like home here. Seriously, you can jump from any DS game right to S&S (Hm, not to yourself, do not abbreviate the name of this game to SS…) and the only thing that will change will be graphics and perspective. So, we have combat focused on timing your dodge, parry and managing your stamina, as well as making yourself familiar with enemies behaviour and attack patterns. Then we have limited healing and opportunity to lose all our gained experience (here named Salt… duh!) upon dying, with only one chance to pick it up again. Plus RPG stats like strength, dexterity or endurance, and stupid amount of gear that can be upgraded, as well as the magnitude of items to use. Did I describe Dark Souls or Salt and Sanctuary now?   So, is this copy-pasted formula bad? No, not exactly. There is this cool sentence: “Imitation is the highest form of flattery”. All of the mentioned above works and is good game design, but at the same time, If I want to play DS, I just boot up DS, not some 2D copy of the game. Even when we look at S&S from the metroidvania angle, there is unfortunately not enough taken from the genre, and what we have here, is simply generic. Yes, we have an interconnected world with whooping 23 different zones, but then you realize that you can beat each area in maybe 20-30 minutes, with backtracking, looking for secrets and hidden items. And your unlockable movement abilities are limited. You can flip gravity, so you run on the ceiling, but it's very situational, because you can perform it only in designated places, not when you wish. After that we unlock wall jump, ability to disable energy fields blocking our way and summon platforms from mist — again, only in specific places — plus mid-air dash. Nothing more, and guess what? It is never utilized properly. The moments that you need to backtrack and gain access to some previously unreachable place are few and far between to even encourage you to do so, especially when rewards are some item or ring you will never use or piece of armor that you CANNOT use because of your stats.  Speaking of which, let’s talk about character progression in this game. The core is standard for souls-like — you defeat the enemy, you get Salt, the number of Salt reaches a certain threshold, you get a level. But then… You know Path of Exile maybe? In this game there is an enormous passive skill tree that confuses everyone. Yup, Salt and Sanctuary have similar things, but for all your core attributes. So you level up, you get your attribute point (here called black pearls) and then you unlock nodes on the stat tree. But hey, there is more! The ability to use weapons and armors is a gatekeeper by your attributes. And I am not talking about meeting requirements of certain strength or dexterity. Each weapon has assigned a tier to it, like for example Sword class 2. You need to unlock the node of Sword class 2 to be able to use this weapon. At first it looks intriguing, but then you realize that you basically lock yourself out from using different gear. Oh, so you put all your points to unlock the path to swords class 5 and then you found a nice great axe? Cool, you cannot use it, because you need to unlock 8 or 9 nodes to unlock great axe class 4 skill. Have fun grinding. It's even more infuriating when you realize that to increase your number of healing items you need to unlock nodes — and it's only one of two ways to do it! WHY?!   What? Resetting your character? Nope, you cannot, dude. You can use an item called Gray pearl to undo ONE NODE. There is no way to reset your entire character back to zero. So if you choose those damn swords, you are forced to use them until the end of the game or waste more hours on grinding. And guess what? Some gear is definitely better than others.  I think this is a good segway to talk about difficulty. It is entirely dependable on what gear you choose to use. I played this game initially around 2018, there is still my old character in save files, so I checked it and it turns out I played with axes. I remember I had a bad time playing that way. Now, replaying for 100% achievements, I decided to use the most braindead tactics, so I just picked a greatsword with light armor for maximum dodge. And I absolutely steamrolled the entire game — even the last boss took me literally THREE tries to beat him. First boss, the Sodden Knight is apparently a great filter for new players, and I beat him on the first try. With a regular sword, because the area where you find a great sword is after him. And mind you, I have no skill, I brute force every soul-like. I didn’t use any magic, but if this game so closely imitates DS, then using magic here is a true easy mode. Nevertheless greatswords are broken, and devs know this, because in the game world there is literally ONE of those swords to find, hidden in a secret location. You can also buy it, but you need to level up a specific creed — more about it later. The rest of them are only obtainable after transmuting boss ashes (just like boss weapons from DS). The Sodden Knight greatsword carried me through the entire game.   Now, little about the mechanic that is unique to this game — sanctuaries and creeds. In DS you have bonfires and firekeepers, you trusty Andre and vendors like, ekhm… Patches. In S&S you have Sanctuaries that are checkpoints, as well as hubs for your vendors. What is more intriguing, you can freely choose what vendor will be in a certain place. When travelling through the world, you can collect figurines of blacksmiths, traders, mages, clerics, etc. You can use them to summon one specific person to a designated sanctuary. Theoretically you have four slots in each place, but practically only three, because there is a guide that allows you to fast travel between sanctuaries, so it’s no brainer to pick him every time. Plus each vendor grants you passive bonuses like better attack power, more salt and money (because yes, aside from salt you have simple coins that you spend on buying regular items) in the corresponding zone around the checkpoint.  Then we had Creeds, a mechanic explicitly tied to Sanctuaries. Imagine covenants from DS, but much, much more sophisticated, focusing solemnly on aiding the player. First of all, each Creed has a different healing item. Some can heal you better, but with drawbacks (like drink too much gold vine at once and you will get poisoned) and others have weaker, but with some extra buffs. Plus each creed merchant, blacksmith mages and clerics have different gear you can buy. But here is the kicker — you need to increase your devotion within the organization to gain access to certain items. Like mentioned above, a basic great sword is only obtainable when you increase your devotion to level 2 within The Three or The Iron Ones. And how do you level up your devotion? By…ehhh… by grinding. Each level demands different enemy's parts, like ears or teeth, and said parts are random drops from bad guys. Yay, more wasting time, this is what we love, this is what we live for, right fellas?  <check the comment section for the rest>

unsuccessfully mashes together aspects of multiple much better games. Does nothing particularly well. Controls are sluggish and non-intuitive so re-mapping keybinds is required because whomever designed the controls has never actually played a game before and seemed to assign buttons at random. Bosses are boring damage sponges that take two or three tries to beat for the most part.  Needs a map. Confusing level layout in the style of a metroidvania without any of the things that actually make those kinds of games approachable. Generally, I hate having to consult a wiki to figure out what anything is or what anything does. This works in some respects, but for things like brands and not explaining anything about what they actually do so you have to look it up is lazy.  Plot and style are try-hard mid 2000's angsty teenage style. This game might as well come with black eyeliner and those red and black striped arm sleeved girlfriend that smells like wet cigarettes. Bloom is not artistic. It's painful on the eyes and washes everything else out. Trash.

Very fun, atmospheric, and addicting 2D souls-like. Before writing this review, I completed a playthrough as a nudist paladin - I never wore a single piece of clothing or armor for the entire playthrough - and it was actually easier than my first playthrough as a knight who used greatswords. Dodging is just too OP. Shields can be pretty OP, too... but require a lot of level investment. Meanwhile, dodging only costs stamina.  I have nitpicks and frustrations with this game, to be sure. For example, for a game with fall damage and penalized deaths, ledge grabbing should NOT be this inconsistent/unpredictable, and it would be great if not every f&%king jump was at the very limit of your jump arc or reach. Give me a freakin' break, man! Gravity killed me more than the bosses! There's also *no in-game map.* Not even slightly. This is the kind of game that NEEDS a map, too. You'll forget about so many areas you need to return to once you unlock certain abilities. Vigil, also a 2D souls-like with platforming, [i]has a map[/i]. I realize Vigil came out way after this game, but Super Metroid came out like 20 years before this and you know what it had? [b]A MAP![/b]  But even the frustrations are not enough to stop me from wanting to play over and over again. I honestly wish there was more to this game. I could play for days on end! If you don't check it out, you're missing out. Just... watch out for tripwires starting when you get to the Village of Smiles. They can really ruin your mood. You [i]will[/i] memorize where they are after a while - they're that annoying - but once you do, they stop being a threat.

Man, this game is so strange for so many reasons. The movement isn't good, the hitboxes aren't good, the variety of weapons that are great versus garbage isn't good, the classes aren't balanced, the bosses aren't very good for the most part, and not having a map in a Metroidvania is insane.  And yet, when taken together, this game is incredibly good. Incredibly. It's a wonderful experience that hits the sweet spot between challenging and frustrating like almost no Souls game or Metroidvania I've played. The interconnection of the world is among the best I've experienced, and the exploration feels more rewarding than in most titles of the same genre.  I'm sad I waited so long to play it. If you have $15 or whatever and you like Dark Souls and Hollow Knight and wandering an incredibly cursed land until you're angry, this is 100% the game for you :)

遊戲前期屎一樣的手感，反人類的機制。這手感應該是我玩過遊戲最差的一款，和 最後信仰 有得比。 原來正統的2D魂是這樣的? 幹...真的魂，但操作手感真的是一坨屎，就算看攻略也沒比較好吞。 那些早期不看攻略玩的肯定0的傾向很嚴重...@@  評價以後有改觀再說  前期打完一些進度後感覺真的沒像人說的那麼神，拿來和空洞騎士相提並論稱神作是過譽了... 或許在早期發行時能算吧...但放到現在來看的，真的算不上神作，頂多算佳作吧...如果沒這屎一般的手感或許會更好...但如果沒這味...那遊戲難度可能就崩了... 本作這所謂的魂味，就真的是這屎味，可能這種要死不活的手感才配稱的上正統魂... 但說真的本作除了殘廢般的操作手感和一堆惡意陷阱加上殘廢的跳躍一直摔死外，其他主要戰鬥部分卻根本沒多魂，基本就是滾來滾去偷個幾刀就結束了 如果這能叫魂，那空洞騎士的BOSS檔次才真叫魂上天，問題是精確格檔彈反呢?    不是搞個動作大延遲就能叫神作魂吧?   前期都打完幾隻BOSS了，反而最魂的是一隻小怪銅質騎士? 只有這隻需要精確盾反...而且判定時機謎的要死，盾反操作手感也很差...  可能本作真的算是個好遊戲吧，以發行早期時的角度來看，沉浸感什麼的也還不錯，上手後是會讓人想繼續完成遊戲啦，但我想我大概率是不會推薦...如果想折磨自己的話，有更多作品是能既折磨又能更爽的...  還有...真的不要拿空洞騎士來比較啦，檔次真的差太多了，本作我看連終結者莉莉系列都很難一拚，瀆神和最後信仰之類的也贏不過，更別提ORI和空洞騎士了，甚至連Grime都比這好玩...  本作最大的優點可能就是對於喜歡看文本的人更有沉浸感吧，氛圍營造的不錯，以老遊戲來說頗具原創性，但其他的就別提了...把它當神作簡直是笑話!  有人說他比空洞神? 到底神在哪? 空洞一隻遊戲前期的3螳螂boss就屌打本作所有boss了...  嚴格來說本作應該和終結者莉莉(1代)在差不多的水平，手感打擊感和那種讓人厭煩的延遲感上相當接近... 但莉莉的戰鬥系統更多元，還有多種武器技能...主角顏值天差地遠...還有啥好說的... ------------------------------------------------------------------------ 1周目打完後總結：本作就是個沉浸感良好的輕鬆小品，當然如果你喜歡不看攻略一直摔死的話就另當別論了。  本作2周目前 最魂的BOSS就只有 開頭劇情殺的難言深淵 (早期能用BUG回頭打的不算) 而且也不是真的多難 就是環境光影和搖晃干擾很噁心 就這樣了   2周目boss人體樹後有幾隻boss難度指數型上升 boss血變超厚 容錯率極低 即便重裝加金黃酒一樣能被連招秒掉....這時才開始有真正魂遊的感覺   如果只看本作是10年前只由兩個人來開發的產物，確實是一個好作品，但還是一句話，別和空洞騎士相提並論... 個人認為這作品唯二比空洞強的部份就氛圍創造沉浸感，然後主角人物造型完勝，畢竟一隻蟲子真的很難有啥美感...  總評分(算上多周目)大概8/10吧   大特價時入手划算

Excellent adaptation of Dark Souls into a 2-D platforming metroidvania. If you are a fan of both of those genres you will probably enjoy this game.       The character stats, equipment, and level up systems are all ripped straight from Dark Souls, but simplified a bit. I literally played through this game with one of my favorite Dark Souls builds, a strength focused, two-handed great sword with holy spells. Like the souls games, there is NO MAP which I could see being jarring for most metroidvania fans. However, like the Souls games, this proves that if the levels are well designed, a map is an unnecessary crutch that distracts from a sense of accomplishment from learning to navigate the atmospheric world. You will get lost down a rabbit hole, you don’t know where it leads, and maybe won’t be able to make it back to spend your souls. This is the special sauce that makes these types of games so engaging for me. There is fast travel between bon fires, and even a covenant system.       On the metroidvania side of things, this game demands quite a bit of platforming skill from the player, and you can expect areas to unlock abilities like wall jumping and air-dashes. The majority of my deaths were from falling. The bosses have pretty cool designs, but this is probably the weakest element of the game. They all feel similar mechanic wise, but there are some fun creative boss moves here and there that will knock your socks off the first time you encounter them, I am looking at you Witch of the Woods. Overall, I really like this game a lot and highly recommend it to fans of challenging action RPGs.

I don't think S&S quite lives up to the standard of a 'must play' title, but its not far from it either. Players will be met with a satisfying reimagining of a souls-like in a 2D space. There are some pretty nice quirks specific to this title like the mini base-builder element at every sanctuary but there are a whole mess of systems that I know I under-utilised because of poor communication in-game between the devs and the players; Whenever I unlocked a new brand, for example, I would always have to google how to actually use it. I was able to brute force my way through with a buffing, 2h, rolling character but I know there is a world of depth available for someone willing to sit and plan out their skill tree, learn the covenants, grind for maxxed gear; they'd just also have to be willing to wade through a bunch of community guides first and that's where this game falls short for me.

20来个小时算是打完一周目了，其实我很少写游戏评价但是这款还是想写一点，感觉一路打过来都快憋出内伤了。  先说剧情，因为并没看的太细，我属于只知道大概讲的什么，深层一概不知。你游剧情就是主线基本毛都没讲，对话里藏一点，物品描述藏一点，技能树的文本太多我基本没看，但那边估计也藏了一点。非常碎，只能说，很魂？正如前面说的剧情我读的不深，所以不过多评价。  然后进入主题，玩法。先说我用的武器，因为本人偏爱轻武器，开局是玩捡的小匕首加盾，后来打飞天怪实在蛋疼就逛了一圈社区，换了大剑玩。本人打游戏的习惯是一周目不看攻略，我也坚持这一点一直到了微笑女皇，这时你游对我最恶心的点就出来了，什么恶意设计，角落堆粪怪，逆天快慢刀加起来我感觉杀伤力没这点大——没有地图。在我解锁的所有地区兜了三圈找到四个门不能从这一侧打开后我终于是妥协了，在盒里找了个地图才找着该往哪去。 但是，当我打完独眼巨兽我发现事情并没有这么简单。即使有地图我也没法一眼辨别哪里是进度锁，至少见过一次才能分辨，还是得跑过去看，这又不得不提你游拿到烙印不教怎么用必须自己进包里看了。我一直到拿到冲刺才发现包里有一页是放烙印的，在那里才有教怎么用。前面的我全都是试出来的。。。 以及怪物设计，也是一言难尽，boss基本是两三招循环，完全没有变招，打到最后发现死的最多的是开局的潮湿骑士；小怪一半高伤害一半有抓取，如果几只一起还能压你起身，怪群里挨一下抓取可以直接等死，漆黑森林那个神人马头三只一起更是处决接处决摸到直接摁死。 再说主角，性能比想象中强，翻滚还算灵活，只是要压负重。本来开局是感觉盾很轮椅的，换了大剑才发现翻滚也不差。不过武器之间的差距。。。嗯，算是平衡没做好吧。  最后评地图设计，跳跳乐难度不高但偏多，堆怪不算严重但略粪，音乐很少但还算好听。那个滤镜是很过分的，看到说是为了贴合设定，但是在你游材质设计并不优秀的情况下再上一层模糊，直接导致了有些地方根本看不出哪是墙哪是地，这点体验是非常差的。 那么在这一点的基础上，你游还完全没有引导，最多只有npc会跟你讲两句他要去哪。也就是告诉你个地区名字而已并不会给你指路。并且那全部都是支线，唯一主线npc他是没有引导性对话的。直接导致了你游引导全靠漂流瓶，也就是这游戏好人多，瓶里写的基本都是真的。好几次我抱着被骗就掉条命的心态去听瓶子的跳坑，结果下面真的都有宝箱。感谢老资历的馈赠。  总而言之，这款游戏玩下来我其实是不太推荐的。建议是可以云一遍，自己玩要做好心理准备。

Hoje em dia, [b]existem muitos Soulsvanias melhores[/b] que Salt and Sanctuary (Hollow Knight e Blasphemous, por exemplo), mas, por ser pioneiro (ou um deles), este merece consideração. Não é um jogo ruim, de forma alguma, embora tenha pontos fracos, mas que são compensados pelos fortes.         A história se resume em você começar tentando salvar uma princesa que estava escoltando rumo a um casamento arranjado, mas acaba preso em uma ilha criada com memórias roubadas por um deus incompleto. No meio do caminho, você nem sabe mais se a princesa é da realeza ou uma escrava...         Depois de horas e horas morrendo, realizando grind, matando monstros e “deuses”, você percebe que a princesa não tem importância nenhuma na trama (isso se você ainda lembrar dela). Mata mais, morre mais por algumas horas, derrota o Deus Sem Nome e precisa decidir entre escapar da ilha ou assumir o lugar dele.  [h3]Pontos Positivos[/h3] - O jogo tem um estilo artístico no qual os personagens parecem bonecos de papel articulados, e os cenários são desenhados a mão com profundidade por camadas. Gosto de uma proposta diferente, que dá identidade visual própria a obra.  - Apesar da história não ter me chamado muita atenção, gostei bastante do conceito do Deus Sem Nome criar a ilha com memórias dos náufragos, prendê-los em um ciclo, assim como também fez com outros deuses... deuses estes que parecem mais legítimos, por serem adorados e haver cultos em seus nomes.  [h3]Pontos Negativos[/h3] - Existe uma conquista que exige o item "Escrito Sangrento" (Bloody Writ) para desbloquear uma área, e não é difícil de obter. Porém, para mim, houve um bug em que, mesmo fazendo tudo corretamente, o item não dropava.   [b]Solução (no meu caso):[/b] descansei no santuário que ia profanar, voltei ao menu inicial e carreguei o jogo. A partir daí, o problema foi resolvido. Obviamente, havia backup do save.  - A movimentação é meio engessada, então exige adaptação.  - Sem mapa = muito tempo perdido.

Plusy: + Klimat nożem kroić - ręcznie rysowana, zamglona oprawa graficzna i oszczędna ścieżka dźwiękowa sprawiają, iż zwiedzanie wyspy budzi uczucie niepokoju rodem z filmów grozy; + Głębia systemu progresji - imponujące (dosłownie) drzewko rozwoju połączone z szeregiem "soulsowych" klas startowych  daje ogrom swobody w kreowaniu postaci; + Piękna architektura poziomów - skróty, ukryte przejścia i sposób, w jaki świat zapętla się wokół siebie, zasługuje na słowa uznania. Eksploracja to czysta przyjemność, nagradzająca wytrwałość i pomysłowość gracza; + Wymagający bossowie - większość antagonistów jest unikalna i wymaga opanowania mechanik rozgrywki oraz nauki zestawów ataków bossa; + Platformowy sznyt - implementacja piętn przyznających nowe umiejętności ruchu (np. chodzenie po suficie) odświeża zabawę w późniejszych etapach, dodając grze metroidwaniovego zacięcia;  Minusy: - Orientacja w terenie - brak mapy (potęgujący imersję) sprawia, iż musimy polegać na sobie przeszukując Wyspę. Niestety wiele korytarzy wygląda tutaj niemal identycznie przez co łatwo się zgubić i wpaść w pułapkę backtrackingu; - Czytelność walki - przy większych przeciwnikach lub efektach czarów, nasza drobna postać czasem znika pod modelem wroga prowadząc do niesprawiedliwych zgonów, spowodowanych nawarstwianiem się animacji; - Zaburzony balans - niektóry oręż (głównie wielkie miecze, topory) ma zdecydowaną (wręcz absurdalną) przewagę obrażeń i zasięgu w porównaniu do magii czy bronii zręcznościowych, dowodząc, iż nie opłaca się z nich korzystać; - Nadmierny minimalizm - lore świata momentami wydaje się zbyt mętny, prezentowany w strzępach dialogów, historii postaci niezależnych i opisów przedmiotów, co utrudnia emocjonalną więź z losem protagonisty i miejscem do którego trafił; - Raz jest łatwo, a raz nie - bolączka skokowego poziomu trudności, wynikająca z nagromadzenia kilku irytujących przeciwników w pozbawionym mobilności miejscu, zaburza odbiór wybitnie zaprojektowanych poziomów.  Ocena: 9/10

O gameplay é onde ele realmente brilha. É basicamente um “Dark Souls em 2D”, com combate mais cadenciado, gerenciamento de stamina e aquele cuidado constante pra não se expor demais. Cada luta exige atenção, principalmente contra chefes.  A variedade de builds é um ponto muito forte. Dá pra jogar com armas pesadas, magia, adagas rápidas, escudo… e o jogo realmente muda dependendo do caminho que você segue. Isso dá uma liberdade muito boa pra experimentar.  A progressão também é bem viciante. Subir de nível, melhorar equipamentos e ir avançando mesmo depois de morrer várias vezes dá aquela sensação clássica de recompensa do gênero.  O mapa é interconectado, estilo metroidvania, o que deixa a exploração bem interessante. Aos poucos você vai entendendo como tudo se conecta, abrindo atalhos e descobrindo áreas novas.  Por outro lado, ele pode ser meio punitivo em alguns momentos, até mais do que o necessário. Tem partes que parecem difíceis mais pela quantidade de inimigos ou armadilhas do que pelo desafio em si.  Visualmente é mais simples, com um estilo meio sombrio e até um pouco “cru”, o que pode não agradar todo mundo, mas funciona dentro da proposta.  O que mais me pegou foi essa mistura de gêneros funcionando tão bem. Ele consegue pegar a essência dos soulslike e adaptar pra 2D sem perder o peso das decisões e do combate.  Acaba sendo aquele tipo de jogo que te desafia, te frustra às vezes, mas quando você pega o ritmo, fica difícil largar.

Joguei este jogo não muito depois de seu lançamento como meu primeiro souslike, e digo que não encontrei um melhor até hoje. Salt and Sanctuary incorporou perfeitamente o core dos jogos da fromsoftware aos moldes metroidvania, tendo a sua dificuldade composta tanto pelo combate estratégico e gestão de recursos dos souslikes, quanto os clássicos problemas de plataforma dos metroidvanias; resultando em um jogo bem difícil e punitivo, que leva a gameplay souslike a dinâmicas muito interessantes, visto que você já podia pular e realizar ataques aéreos aqui muito antes de Elden Ring. Por esta razão, "Salt" é frequentemente chamado de "Dark Souls 2D", mas eu lhe garanto que definitivamente não se resume a esta alcunha;   Isso porque "Salt" possuí uma penca de mecânicas centrais que divergem bastante dos jogos da Fromsoftware, como por exemplo, os credos: uma mecânica de facção bem legal e única, onde seu credo/religião lhe dá acesso a recompensas bem variadas (que podem influenciar bastante o seu estilo de jogo); uma arvore de habilidades bem grande e flexível (parecida com a de Path of Exile, ainda que bem menor e mais simples); crafting de equipamento utilizando loot de monstros e bestiário (provavelmente inspirado em Monster Hunter, mas novamente, bem mais simples); progressão metroidvania (mapas interligados, segredos, poderes que dão acesso a novos lugares)... estes elementos diversos foram tão bem polídos e implementados, sem quebrar o ritímo souslike, que resultou em uma experiência única e uma gameplay bem gostosa, satisfatória e boa de revisitar devido ao seu fluxo e fator replay. Vale mencionar também o modo co-op, certamente uma das melhores experiência de cooperação que eu já tive na vida! Extremamente divertido e deixa o sistema de invocações da Fromsoftware no chinelo, pois permite que você jogue a campanha inteira com um amigo de forma orgânica.  Dito tudo isso, eu ainda destaco a lore, arte, e atmosfera bem misteriosas e sombrias; os npcs carísmaticos com quests e diálogos bem envolventes; as boss fights variadas, maravilhosas e desafiadoras, e... claro, o level design enorme, insanamente criativo, interligado e cheio de segredos sendo o principal charme do jogo e o que faz dele tão especial e marcante. Obra de gênio!

只有在这款游戏中我才能找到2d魂游的答案。  第一次上手这款游戏，你可能会有很多魂系列的既视感，包括但不限于门无法从这一侧打开，持续战斗会减少最大血条和精力上限，巧妙的地图设计，以及一板一眼（玩家读boss指令）的boss战。  我很想说这就是我想玩的2d魂游，但是我又很难说这就是我想要玩的2d魂游的最终形态。  盐1是我最喜欢的类银河恶魔城游戏，甚至超过了丝之歌。  在奥日中缺少的战斗你可以在这里找到，在空洞骑士中缺少的探索奖励你也可以在这里找到，跳跳乐，前面的区域以后再来探索吧……等等等等，我还是不敢想象这个游戏仅仅是由两个人加上一只猫制作的。  如果真的让我去给找个游戏找扣分点我可以找到很多，绝对比打差评的还多，但是它的优点我实在拒绝不了。  10/10.希望有一天我可以玩到比盐1更好的2d魂like游戏，又或是更加优秀的类银河恶魔城游戏。

毋庸置疑的经典！[strike]虽然是第一次接触这种游戏[/strike] 之前看博主玩过，也没怎么看，某天想起突然买来玩，结果就停不下来了 目前正在玩二周目！我很喜欢这作的所有美术和音效音乐，整体的故事氛围也烘托的很有意思 以下内容含有二周目开头剧透： [spoiler]交谈时npc的对话变得突然轻松戏谑了起来，之后的其他npc也突然说出一些像是meta游戏那样打破第四面墙的感觉。。。这让我感到非常震撼！一周目的时候我就因为旅途路上有他们的陪伴而感到温暖（角色的对话描写虽然很少，却让我感觉他们都是活生生的人），二周目整的这一出就更感觉他们是真的人似的了[/spoiler] 武器和天赋点系统还有boss设计我都非常喜欢！！！我第一把用的是大锤和大剑，很顺畅，同时也开了另外好多个不同开局的角色体验别的武器，我非常喜欢这样多样化和组合连招的设计！boss设计我觉得也很合理，招式在有趣的同时不会称得上太难，就算是纯匕首打多打个十几二十遍的也能打过，而且这样一次次重复训练的过程我个人也是很喜欢的！感觉这整个游戏我都是怎么说怎么喜欢的（除了森林那边那个独角兽） 感谢工作室制作这个游戏！！它拯救了我的电子阳痿

[h2]探索有趣，動作爽快，難度適中的2D橫版類魂+類銀--《鹽與聖所》全成就一週目個人感想[/h2]  昨晚23點出頭，筆者十分愉悅地完成了《鹽與聖所》的一週目，達成了全成就。這幾天基本上都是2個小時起步，“泡”在這個“小而美”的絕讚2D手繪畫風“類魂”遊戲裡：有發現隱藏寶物的喜悅，開通地區間捷徑的豁然開朗，艱難打贏BOSS後的滿足，當然也有為自己的失誤而產生的懊惱。但總的來說，筆者可以說是經歷了一場酣暢淋漓的戰鬥之旅，這14個小時全程無尿點，更不拖沓，雖有挫折但都能找到辦法從而一一克服。因此，強烈推薦這款《鹽與聖所》給喜歡魂類遊戲/銀河惡魔城遊戲的諸位；另外，個人也覺得這款蠻適合作為“魂類”遊戲的入門款，從而推薦給想要嘗試這個類型，但又怕難度過高而中途放棄的新玩家。  先來說遊戲的缺點： 1.遊戲沒有內置地圖，所以如果純自己摸索著玩，樂趣的確會更多，但也更容易產生“我是誰，我在哪，我要做什麼”的迷茫。好在遊戲已發售多年，海內外的社區大神們早就人工繪製出了這遊戲的地圖，還是非常詳實的版本--所有的寶物，所有的BOSS，所有的路線都一目了然（中文版、英文版）。但這依舊無損這個遊戲的探索樂趣，反而會讓人驚歎製作組的匠心獨具：所有區域都是無縫連接，不需要加載，更沒有黑屏切換；有的，只有四通八達，豁然開朗的“曲徑通幽”。 2.剛開始玩的時候，會覺得遊戲畫面有一種“霧蒙蒙，模糊看不清”的感覺。這個是遊戲特色，是製作組刻意為之的，契合遊戲劇情，而且基本上只要玩下去就會慢慢習慣。 3.整體遊戲畫面偏暗，有些地方甚至全黑。但這個也是遊戲特色，製作組還根據這點設計了各種利用場景背景的怪物，場地陷阱。不過這點在遊戲中是能夠找到解決辦法的，且不止一種：前期就能獲得火把，商店NPC處可以購買吊掛在武器上的“照明護符”，咒文裡也有“光亮”咒文，裝備裡也有一枚戒指可以提升光照水平。以上解決手段各有各的優點，也各有各的缺點，全看玩家自己抉擇。  再來說說遊戲的優點： ①.這個安裝體積不到1GB的遊戲，地圖的整體性非常出色。大部分區域在擊敗區域BOSS後，都能開啟通往之前區域的捷徑，有些地區還甚至不止一條。這就讓探索的樂趣大增，因為不經意間你就發現自己兜兜轉轉又回到了“熟悉”的地方。加上，探索給的獎勵也都是有用的，雖然可能不是馬上就能派上用場，但真到了要用的時候，翻找下“背包”發現自己已經擁有時的小驚喜還是時不時的會出現在遊玩過程中作為調劑。 ②和《艾爾登法環》一樣的降低難度的機制：暫時打不過BOSS的話，可以退一步海闊天空，去農經驗農素材變強，不用硬著頭皮死磕。本作開場可選8個職業出生，但都共用一個天賦樹，僅僅是每個職業的在這個天賦樹上的起點不同。因而要是玩家願意，完全是能夠把自己的角色打造成“六邊形”全能戰士的。 ③本作的死亡挫敗感沒那麼強烈。雖然沿用了魂類遊戲的通用設定：死亡掉魂（本作則是鹽）。且本作同樣的，角色死亡後會掉落身上所有的“鹽”（經驗），但實際遊玩途中，挫折感並沒有那麼強烈：因為一來你可以回到剛才死亡的地方擊敗導致你死亡的怪物拿回所掉落的全部的“鹽”（如果是BOSS戰，那削減一定量的BOSS血量後就能拿回；如果是地形殺，那會在死亡的地點生成一隻很容易擊殺的鹽蝙蝠），只有在沒拿回丟失的鹽的情況下二次死亡才會真正產生損失。二來就是因為本作裡“鹽”的入手渠道多種多樣（可刷怪獲取，BOSS戰打贏會給大量，也能用金幣去NPC商店處購買鹽袋子/鹽箱子獲取），且都給的非常大方，不摳搜。因此，除非你是攢了一大筆鹽沒用掉（鹽的主要功用：人物升級時的經驗，武器升級/改造時的貨幣），那才會有可能感覺到“心痛”。 ④本作的動作性很好，武器種類豐富（16種），且所有近戰武器都有連招系統，獨立的魔法系統/咒文系統都很有特色，至於架盾防禦，全武器招架彈反（你甚至可以反向招架）更是一個都被沒落下。所以同一把武器，在高手和新手的手上是截然不同的樣貌，前者是能在戰鬥中打出極具觀賞性效果的。因此也造就了本作內所有武器都是上手門檻很低，“易學難精”的存在，畢竟要精通其中的所有門道，還是要下不少精力和功夫的。由此可見本作這套動作係統的深度也是足夠的。 ⑤本作的劇情故事“謎語人”程度較低，只要你沒有錯過和劇情NPC的對話，打完全流程基本上就能弄明白這個故事的梗概：入島--找人--深入--真相--結局。當然也少不了的“魂類”遊戲傳統：把零碎的額外信息藏在物品簡介裡。 ⑥之所以在文章的開頭說難度適中，是因為這遊戲，製作組是有給到“官方輪椅”的--“大劍+黃金酒”。這個組合傷害輸出很是暴力，因此可以幫助新手“莽”穿一切BOSS，開啟“EASY模式”。加上遊戲也有給到NG+，一週目“莽”穿一切的新手在有了對遊戲全貌的了解後，後續的二週目是會有想要嘗試下其他那些“琳瑯滿目”的武器的，至少筆者本人是這麼想的。 ⑦網上有不少“保姆式”的詳實開荒視頻，可以幫助新手更好地適應遊戲，降低挫折感，體會到《鹽與聖所》獨有的樂趣。本次筆者把這款遊戲撿起來，就是因為找到了這麼一套用心的視頻攻略。筆者個人是覺得“魂類”遊戲跟著攻略視頻走，也依舊不會破壞其獨有的樂趣，畢竟哪怕就算知道《法環》裡的女武神招式，知道該如何正確的應對她的招式，又有多少人能夠一次就搞定女武神的？不照樣被這超強的BOSS虐的死去活來的？ 但提前知道相關信息，的確會有助於玩家增加挑戰時的信心，更好地總結失敗經驗，從而最終打敗那些強敵，獲取“我做到了”的成就感。 ⑧而且BOSS的攻略方法不止一種，給到了玩家足夠的自主選擇權。你可以跟著攻略視頻用“逃課”方法莽過去，也可以在探索這個區域中找到製作組留下的“恰好”針對BOSS的防禦盾牌，抗性戒指，從而減少面對BOSS時的難度，減少“一時失誤”時所付出的代價。 ⑨遊戲鼓勵玩家多週目遊玩（多流派的差異性和多個教派的特殊物品都別具特色），且製作組在遊戲發售後依舊有在持續更新：各種BUG修正不提，其中2023年增加了“強化模式”，2025年還追加了“隨機模式”。前者算是官方的加載了平衡性補丁的修正版（加強其他武器，略微削弱原版過於強力的武器），讓遊戲的可玩性更加的出色。而後者--“隨機模式”則是專門給高手們挑戰自我所準備的：畢竟全地圖怪物隨機出現的模式，一個運氣不好，初始避難所前遇到大後期怪物而陷入苦戰的可能性也是存在的。  以上，就是筆者跟著開荒視頻打完《鹽與聖所》一週目後的些許感想。這遊戲雖然體量不大，但“麻雀雖小，五臟俱全”，“魂類”遊戲/“銀河惡魔城”遊戲愛好者都能從中找到屬於自己的樂趣。只要不怕失敗，畢竟本作失敗的懲罰是真的蠻低的，個人是推薦有興趣的諸位不妨都來嘗試下這款遊戲的。而其常年在steam打折季以2.5折的價格出售，僅需109TWD。這個價格換一次至少持續10+小時的冒險之旅，怎麼想，都是不虧的。

Игра совмещающая в себе механики метроидвании и соулайков.  По классике жанра соулсов мы просыпаемся на корабле, а после битвы с боссом-туториалом, просыпаемся на пляже и не понимаем что происходит и куда идти, впрочем игра относительно линейна все таки элементы метроидвании не дадут пройти дальше без открытия определенных навыков (будто двойной прыжок или изменение гравитации).  Игра имеет весьма удручающую атмосферу, по всюду упадок, кровь и монстры, многие из боссов имеют достаточно интересный и даже пугающий дизайн: Королева Улыбок и Л̶о̶ж̶е̶ ̶Х̶а̶о̶с̶а̶  Древо Людей до сих пор порой вспоминаю их довольно стремный (в хорошем смысле) дизайн. Жаль только что Лор игры довольно слабый.  SaS имеет весьма специфический стиль немного отдающий дешевизной (чем-то напоминает Южный Парк), но игре это пожалуй идет даже в плюс, так как более такого стиля я нигде не видел.  Платформинг в игре весьма приятный и не особо бесит (не считая платформинг над И̶с̶ч̶а̶д̶ь̶я̶м̶и̶ ̶А̶д̶а̶  Стержнезверями, они же Единороги)  В целом гра очень понравилась возможно даже перепройду ее через несколько лет. (Полное прохождение на 100% заняло 21 час)

An incredibly good Soulslike. The 2d perspective makes it a bit more accessible than the genre typically is, although the game is still quite challenging (sometimes frustrating with how frequent death-by-falling is).  Pros: + Deep, challenging gameplay + Atmospheric + Intricate level design + Slightly more accessible than the usual Soulslike + Distinctive character art style that can be strangely endearing  Cons: - Soundtrack is a bit repetitive - Lots of cheap deaths due to platform hell & falling damage.

This game is a rough ride but extreamly satisfying at the same time. Having no map does make traversing throughout areas pretty difficult often times going through the same areas over again when I didn't need to. At the same time I'm leveling, finding new items and shortcuts so not actually a waste of time. The boss fights are really fun. I constantly find myself reevaluating the bosses weakness so I'm equipping new sets of armor to help with the fights. I LOVE THIS. I love making adjustments to persevere when areas get extreamly difficult. I die CONSTANTLY but learning from my mistakes and getting better is all part of the process. This game rocks my dopamine receptors and I could not put it down. This is the best 2D souls like I've ever played.

[h1] 盐与避难所 [/h1] [h1] ——2D版《黑魂》 [/h1] [b] （别称：盐和圣所/大剑与黄金酒/剪刀与黄金酒） [/b]  [h3] 特点总结 [/h3] +看着跟《黑魂》哪哪都不一样、玩着跟《黑魂》哪哪都一样 ·小成本制作、内容不够丰富、平衡性不佳 -跳跃体验较差  [h3] 游玩时长 [/h3] ·约20小时单周目流程 ·双结局  [h3] 介绍 [/h3] 《盐与避难所》在类银河恶魔城玩法中完美融入“魂系”黑暗奇幻的硬核风格，获得了玩家的广发好评。类魂游戏众多，然得其神韵者却是寥寥，而《盐》正是一款把魂系精髓扒了个裤衩儿不都剩的2D横版类魂游戏代表作。  乍看之下，这游戏跟《魂》的外表差了十万八千里：浓烈的意象塑造出中世纪的宗教狂热，游戏中的『避难所』并非温暖的港湾，更像是一座孤寂的教堂，在无尽的黑暗中为你提供片刻喘息。有点脏兮兮的手绘画面为世界赋予了独特的潦草、灰暗的质感，配合上克系风格的诡异生物与压抑的光影，营造出一种粗粝的、属于黑暗时代的真实感。  可一上手，熟悉的魂味儿就会瞬间弥散开来。  游戏摒弃了华丽的连招，极低的角色性能使得每一次翻滚、格挡、攻击都需要精打细算，玩家必须学会在“贪刀即死”的恐惧中寻找间隙。这种机制在2D视角下反而产生了奇特的化学反应。由于缺少了3D场景中的站位，战斗变得更加纯粹且残酷。玩家必须精准判断敌人的攻击前摇与判定范围，很好地还原了《黑暗之魂》那种刀尖上跳舞的的紧张感。  《盐》作为一款横版类银河恶魔城游戏，地图设计并非简单的从左到右的线性推进，而是构建了一个庞大、垂直且环环相扣的迷宫。玩家要频繁地乘坐电梯、推开铁门、踩碎脆弱的木板，解锁连接着天壤之别的捷径。当玩家历经千辛万苦，终于推开一扇铁门回到最初的安全屋时，那种“柳暗花明”的豁然开朗，正是《黑暗之魂》地图设计的精髓所在。  剧情也是魂系的老套路。碎片化的叙事散落在物品描述里、NPC的只言片语中、以及那些你路过时偶尔瞥见的背景细节里。游戏从不讲清楚。愿意琢磨，就能从那些蛛丝马迹里拼出一个不可名状的暗黑寓言；要是不在乎，就一路砍过去，也不耽误通关。  说到『黄金酒』，这游戏的平衡性确实不佳。玩『匕首』或『鞭子』基本等同于给自己上刑『大剑』则非常强势，算是给手残玩家的补偿。 关卡难吗？它不一定卡你很久，但一定把你气得够呛——陷阱藏在视野死角、平台跳跃下面就是即死深渊……然后就有种让人越被虐越上头的魔性。那些饱受硬核调教、被魂系 抽打的老ASS，往往会在通关后越发觉得空虚难耐，那么此游戏就会如同月工塞一样很好的发挥出补充和缓解作用。  20小时一周目，不算长；俩结局， 不算多。它粗糙、偏执、甚至有点不讲道理，但偏偏就还原炉《黑魂》股子犟劲儿，这让《盐》在一堆拿捏不好尺度的类魂游戏里，显得格外对味儿。

Отличное рпг и соулс, с интересным лором и боссами. Сам я прошел игру уже не один раз, плохого класса оружие нет, все может рвать, главное пылесосить для ресурсов для трансмутации, можно получить как оружие боссов, так и рядовых врагов. Из минусов, враги с единственной атакой в виде захвата, их не так много, но они раздражают. А так же, гравитация, прямо как в творениях фромов, но здесь бонусом идет плотформинг. Оружие много, красивых и  разнообразных локаций, боссов не мало, и они уникальны. В общем, для меня отличная игра, которая запоминается. Насчет графики, я быстро привык, дизайн и стиль у игры, мне вполне понравились.

I am a souls like fan as I've played DS1-3, Eldren Ring, Demon Souls, Bloodborne, etc.  For me I play single player games often because I need to pause at a moments notice. This game has no reason to not be able to pause when in strictly offline mode. Also the muted visuals make a lot of attacks or things just hard to see on my laptop. Starting out as a mage with no staff or wand felt odd and made the early game a bit rough. Especially when my spells are worse than my sword by far. Having boss areas be completely non-obvious made for a few deaths where I lost a lot of salt without being able to get it back which was frustrating. Losing gold when you die is also just overly punishing too. Overall if you are looking for a 2D souls like I dont think this is a bad one. It just isn't for me and if you find you're like me I'd spare yourself the time.

Salt é um jogo que me acompanhou em dois momentos distintos da minha vida: no passado como garoto e no presente como adulto.  Compartilho um grande amor por essa franquia, sendo um cara que sempre jogou em um pc ruim até pra rodar GTA San Andreas, eu vi esse jogo como meu primeiro contato real com um Souls Like.   A temática, a história, a maneira com que a história é contada através de "cartas" é muito interessante e torna o jogo vivo!  Sempre que jogo eu fico tentado a passar pelo menos alguns minutos lendo descrição de item ou tentando juntar as historinhas na árvore de habilidades! Um feeling bem legal.  Pra ser bem sincero, revisitei esse jogo pensando que me aproximaria de um velho amigo, mas no fim nossos caminhos se separaram, eu sendo o responsável por isso.  Me pergunto se eu teria um lugar na ilha, não entre aqueles que naufragam nela, mas entre os monstros que nela são aprisionados.  Enfim, se quiser investir no jogo: a platina é bem tranquila e a gameplay é visceral, você sente os ataques, sejam seus ou dos inimigos! O jogo também é repleto de belos cenários e o que mais me impressiona é que, mesmo sendo um metroidvania, ele não passa tanto essa vibe, como se o peso transmitido pela história e estética o separasse de jogos mais vibrantes, como Hollow Knight ou Ori.  Como últimas considerações, não sei ao certo quando vou revisitar esse belo jogo, mas não me preocupo com isso. Foi uma experiência muito boa rejogá-lo e não tenho medo de dizer que faria de novo!

«Этот мир — лишь пепел, брошенный в пасть бесконечного океана». Ты очнешься на берегу. Твои легкие полны соли, а в руках — лишь обломки твоей прошлой веры. Salt and Sanctuary — это не просто игра, это литургия отчаяния, запечатленная в грубом двухмерном рисунке. Оскверненные земли Здесь каждый шаг — это сделка со смертью. Мир Острова соткан из тумана и крови, где архитектура шепчет о забытых богах, а враги — это ожившие кошмары, рожденные из соли и греха. Ты будешь карабкаться по скользким стенам замков и падать в бездны, где свет — редкая милость. Соль и Кровь Твое спасение — Соль. Она — опыт, она — валюта, она — твоя надежда. Потеряй её, и ты станешь лишь еще одной тенью в тумане. Бой — это танец на плахе. Каждое движение весомо. Массивные мечи дробят кости, а заклинания выжигают саму суть бытия. Но помни: усталость — твой верный палач. Святилища — твои редкие вздохи. Здесь ты возносишь молитвы, призываешь союзников и пытаешься склеить свою разбитую душу. Приговор Это суровое подражание великим Souls-играм, ставшее чем-то большим. Мрачная, вязкая атмосфера и глубокое древо талантов заставят тебя возвращаться к жизни снова и снова, пока ты не поймешь: выход не в победе, а в принятии своей участи. Смерть — это не конец. Это лишь новая порция соли для жадной земли.

With several Souls-Likes and a Souls game under my belt, I can safely this game is nice 7.5/10.   It is fun and the game is very atmospheric when it comes to the environments. The world building only happens if you do the quests and read the monsters backstories. Even then, some of the information is environmental and really requires due diligence. There is a moment where, if you are solo, you won't learn how to switch basically your house or guild. Apparently different houses have different bonuses, so go in with the information. ALSO, a map. Go to the wiki for this game or Steam discussion page for the level maps, so at least that way you can get around without going in a circle. Even then you can still navigate slightly blind. I am not sure what the quests would give because I bypassed like three or four areas while reaching the ending. I am not sure how I managed that.   Worse things with this game, PITCHWOODS AND THE SPINDLEBEAST. THEY SUCK. Absolute worse monster ever and worse area. I almost raged quit there. The platforming was hard and I realized I need to get better at. I died so many times to the freaking Kraeken Wrym, 15 times. My word it was rough. It does suck some weapons can only be upgraded and others can't due to the limitations of the final upgrade pieces, but it is doable still.   Grinding wasn't bad once you understand Will=Drop Rate, and where to farm. I love Mal's Palace and Ziggurat of Dust for grinding. I can easily get like 15 shimmering pearls from the Cyrpt Keeper in the Palace after about 4 hours, with a drop rate 60 or something.   Favorite boss was the Nameless God, not sure if it was on purpose or not, but he plays like a 2D Nameless King. Which once I kind got it in my head, 8 tries easy. His patterns was simple but the mix up can be hard. His grab sucks though, I dodge both of his lunges. Even then, I really like his fight. I did play on normal mode so the health pool was different. Shield was so good for his fight too. With a lot of will and high defense to Slashing damaging, he wasn't killing as fast, but my goodness his grab can be a make or break for me.   A lot of good with jarringly huge bad, but if you can mitigate the bad (mainly earlier on and any platforming, again I suck at it is all), the game is a fun time.   Thank you for making the game. :D

Игра прикольная, даёт в меру сложности, есть стиль. Исследовать мир и бродить по нему +- интересно, находить нужные предметы для продвижения дальше по "сюжету" не затруднительно. Прошёл дважды, при том второй раз был с куда большим удовольствием. Минусы есть, к примеру этот же самый "стиль": иногда картинка на столько заблюреная и размытая, что все мелкие детали почти одного тона смешиваются в кашу, глаза режет. Есть баги с атаками противников и боссов, не понял для чего некоторые механики лагеря у точек сохранения, например улучшение уровня своей веры в выбранную религию (или что-то вроде того). Прокачка брони как будто вообще не имеет смысла, как и в целом её ношение: проще всего ходить полностью без одежды/в очень лёгкой экипировке/в частях экипировки и иметь быстрый и далёкий перекат, чем быть неуклюжим танком, которого всё равно пробивают, у которого мизерный и очень медленный перекат. В целом, положительных ощущений больше, чем негативных

Salt and Sanctuary é um jogo relativamente antigo, lançado em meados de 2016, ele tenta adaptar um Soulslike em um multiplataforma 2D. O jogo é bom, tem uma progressão satisfatória, mapa interligado entre cenários diversos, chefes difíceis e mecânicas de combate baseadas em se decorar o move set do adversário, afim de subjugá-lo. Você vai encontrar uma grande variedade de itens, feitiços e equipamentos. Uma hora perceberá que não vai precisar de mais do que um equipamento tier 2 bem upado para zerar o jogo. (Pelo menos foi assim comigo, fiz uma build de força com espada de duas mãos, encontrei um set tier 2 em um cenário inicial e o utilizei até o fim do jogo tranquilamente). Se você busca uma jogatina progressiva, inteligente e com uma pitada de desafios, Salt and Sanctuary será um jogasso em sua biblioteca. Amantes de Soulslike... Experimentem esse jogo, creio que valerá cada centavo gasto. Nota final: Sal/10

评分6.5/10，24h全成就  很有魂味，可以看出来很多借鉴传统黑魂的玩法/梗，跑图难度与BOSS难度设计也有点像魂一，滚筒洗衣机带给你惊吓无限.jpg 尤其在跑图探索上深得黑魂精髓，烙印创新点也不错，同时也不埋没2d横版的特色，大部分怪和魂2一样靠走位就能躲。  扣分主要是扣在以下N点： 1.地图正路引导非常差，传统黑魂虽然岔路多、探索复杂，但正路的引导性和标记性是很强的，不至于转半天转不到BOSS房，盐1的地图由于正副道路主次差异不明显，很容易迷路或者失去探索耐心。 2.无用的捷径系统，黑魂的捷径几乎都是服务于快速重复开BOSS、无法传送或传送不方便时抄近路，但我的游玩体验里盐1的捷径要么完全没用，要么就是只服务于开启BOSS门，开了捷径也没有多少省时省事的喜悦感，建议重打魂1 3.没有亮度设置，N卡面板也不能强制调亮。反复打难言深渊的时候有些招真看不清，很难受。 4.后期跑图难度太低，缺乏大规模精英怪，轮椅武器打过去就完了。

Il Dark Souls definitivo in 2D - un opera impressionante, a tratti più difficile perfino del suo fratello maggiore; l'impossibilità di muoversi a 360° rende alcune schivate veramente difficili da effettuare, complice poi un IA dei nemici aggressiva come poche. Lo spietato combat system è supportato da un intricato albero delle abilità, uno dei più ricchi che ci siano tuttora in circolazione. Inutile indorare la pillola, questo non è un gioco per tutti. L'accessibilità va a vantaggio di chi vuole una sfida tosta e dall'alta imprevedibilità, a scapito della sua salute mentale. Siccome i curiosi ignari troveranno solo una scusante per lasciare il titolo in fretta e furia, il mio consiglio spassionato è di gettarvi su qualcosa di un pochino più semplice prima di affacciarvi a questo abisso senza fine.  C'è un elemento molto positivo da valutare e cioè il fatto che giocando a Salt and Sanctuary avrete modo di allenare molto la pazienza se qualora (come il sottoscritto) ne siete solitamente sprovvisti.

The "Salt" in the title isn't just a clever name, it’s your currency.  You’re shipwrecked on a foggy, grim island where everything is shades of grey and depressing, and you spend many hours getting bullied by bosses that can swat you like a fly. It’s brutal, it’s ugly-pretty, and it’s arguably the most honest 2D Soulslike ever made. If you want a game that respects your intelligence but hates your guts, this is it.

So I'm a bit late to Salt and Sanctuary, I started playing it on April 26th of 2024, so that would be almost 8 years after the games initial Steam release, but even I can tell that this game perfectly encapsulates the era in which it was developed. As usual, this review will avoid containing any spoilers besides major game mechanics.  Salt and Sanctuary really feels like a passion project from the later stages of Newgrounds or some other indie games site in more ways than one. For starters, the amount of content in the game, whether that be the hundreds of unique weapons and armors in the game that allows you to explore many different playstyles. Or the multitude of unique enemies and bosses that you'll find throughout the world of the game, which really make the world feel once lived in and add a lot of atmosphere. As well as the entirely hand-drawn art style, which, while very rough looking, does an amazing job of conveying the intended setting and atmosphere for the game. As for music, the game has next to none outside of the overall boss theme. The music it does have is decent, but this is just something to note. The controls in Salt and Sanctuary are a little unique. If you play with controller, the default controls might not be what you're used to, and this is to be expected when playing a game this old. The default controls are comfortable and intuitive once you get used to them, but they're not what most people are used to, as you're forced to move your character around with the left thumbstick, something I wasn't really comfortable with at first, but if you try to change it, it kind of messes up the intended feel of the game.  Now the world of the game isn't exactly "ginormous", relatively speaking anyway, but due to the lack of a map, any sort of guidance or handholding at all, you get plenty of time to explore the world in your own way throughout your playthrough, and make mental notes of whatever areas you deem to be "important" or "useful". On the topic of handholding, I should note that the only real form of guidance in Salt and Sanctuary comes in the form of user-generated notes from the games "Message in a bottle" system. Similar to the Souls games, players are able to write messages using a limited catalogue of words for other real players to come across and read. Surprisingly, Salt and Sanctuary has a very small but active community all these years later, and these bottles are no rarity to find scattered throughout the world. Now of course, these messages being user-generated could say literally anything, and not be helpful at all, but I've found that the majority of the bottled messages I read throughout my first playthrough were very helpful in finding very hidden secrets, or aiding me in boss encounters.  As for the overall experience, it's a very fun game, it can sometimes be confusing on where and how to make progress, and there are some trolls and difficult sections of the game, but that's where those bottles I mentioned earlier will come in quite handy. The overall difficulty of the game isn't very souls-like at all if I'm honest, if anything it's just a little bit harder than the typical 2D combat platformer style game, not that that's an issue or anything, just something to note if that's what you were looking for. There also aren't very many "unique" game mechanics, at least not by todays standards, however I'm sure some of the current mechanics were explored less when the game first released, though this also isn't necessarily a bad thing, just another thing to note. I found progression in Salt and Sanctuary to be very linear, as in not really feeling too over / underpowered during any stage of the game, though the game does include a leveling system as well as a skill tree, which I suppose could both be easily abused given enough time. I also never found death to be a very big deal in the game because of this, you don't really lose much when you die in this game, and whatever you do lose can be easily reacquired either passively or in 20 minutes max if you're really not playing smart. The story and lore within the game also pretty much entirely failed to make it across to me, but this could possibly be entirely my fault, as I have a history with failing to pick up on story details in other games as well, however I can confidently say that Salt and Sanctuary does not hold your hand when it comes to understanding the world that you're running around in.  With all that being said, I'm giving Salt and Sanctuary an 8/10 for experience, as I found the game very fun and unique, except it seems there was little effort put in to completely balance the game and make it a little bit more challenging the whole way through. And my quality rating for this game is going to be 10/10, as just from a first look you can tell how high effort of a project Salt and Sanctuary really is. I ran into zero bugs or glitches during my entire 19 hour playthrough, and as of writing this review the game still gets semi-regular updates and bugfixes which is just insane to me.  I would recommend this game to ANYONE who's into the Metroidvania genre at all, it's one of the highest quality and most fun experiences I've had with a campaign style game in a while, and if you end up playing the game after reading this review, I hope you enjoy your experience with it!

It’s abundantly clear playing Salt & Sanctuary that the developers took a good, hard look at Dark Souls then attempted to recreate it as faithfully as possible on a 2d-plane. And while they absolutely nailed the general aesthetic and level design, I was less impressed with the imprecise combat and finicky platforming sections.  You’ll begin in true soulslike fashion by picking a starting class and item before being thrust into the thick of things with a nigh-unbeatable tutorial boss that wrecks both you and the ship you are on before you can even quaff a healing potion. You awake on a faraway misty shore; and are immediately pestered by an old man into choosing between one of three possible creeds.   Now, this is where Salt & Sanctuary begins to differentiate itself from its role model, as instead of a single hub you will be creating dozens during your time spent on the island, claiming each empty sanctuary for your creed and populating them with a range of NPCs; from the traditional blacksmith and merchant, to an alchemist who can transmute boss souls into unique weapons or leaders who dole out short collect-a-thon side quests. The more you increase your devotion to a particular creed, the more rewards you can rack up, including extra potions or other consumables. You will also discover additional creeds while exploring, and although I was occasionally tempted by the gold-loving House of Splendor or the Game of Thrones-inspired Iron Ones, I ended up sticking with my starting creed that was the best fit for my cleric build.  Additionally, each NPC adds a passive bonus to the region you are in, so a leader adds a 10% increase to attack for example, whereas the alchemist adds a general defence boost. These NPCs are added to your sanctuary by offering up an effigy found in various locations around the map, though they are plentiful enough that I could fill all my sanctuaries with people by the time I reached the end of the game. The character art is a bit bug-eyed and cartoonish, but the environments on the other hand are, for the most part, stunning. I’m not sure the game gives off a great first impression as the opening areas are rather dull and dark, but once the it opens up you are treated to a much wider colour palate, from the amethyst hues of Hager’s Cavern, to the greens of The Watching Woods or the ethereal blue of Siam Lake.   This is also probably a good place to point out that Salt & Sanctuary is a massive game, with dozens of different locations to explore, and if you enjoy investigating every little nook and cranny, it’ll take you a good thirty hours to complete.   Another positive point is how these environments fit together. I love the feeling in a souls game where you spend hours traversing a dungeon only to unlock a shortcut back to another area, or, even better, to a previously unreachable locked door or sanctuary.  Developers Ska Studios have really nailed this aspect of the game, and even though in typical souslike fashion there is no map, you’ll soon get a rough idea as to how everything fits together.  Levelling up is done through a final fantasy 10-style grid sphere, which was mostly fine, though it was occasionally annoying to be forced to unlock a point in a stat I wasn’t invested in just to be able to wield more weapons or wear better armour.   Unfortunately, where Salt & Sanctuary falls shy of greatness is in the gameplay department. I traditionally gravitate towards heavier weapons, and none of them felt quite right here, with weird hit boxes that meant I sometimes damaged enemies without even touching them, and other times watched my little line of sprites go right through them without damaging them at all. Blocking is finicky too, and I ended up relying on the dodge roll to avoid most attacks, which is a shame.   Item and spell selection is another frustrating hassle. All your spells, items, and healing flasks are mapped to a single bar of icons that you have to scroll through with the directional buttons. This is annoying during exploration, but especially egregious during boss fights where you have to take your eyes off the centre of the screen for a moment to scroll through sometimes up to seven or eight icons to get to the spell you want to use. As I reached the end of the game, I had five buffs and three different potions, and attempting to select them all during the more aggressive boss fights was insane.  And speaking of insanity, the knockback from bosses and some of the larger enemies is crazy. You’ll be sent flying across the screen to land in an undignified heap far far away. This is fine during boss fights, but not fun when you are traversing poison-filled pits, spike traps, and insta-kill holes.   To be clear, I was playing on the  “Expanded” combat mode which was introduced in a later version of the game and is now selected by default, it’s possible the original, or “classic” combat was very different, although earlier reviews don’t lend much credence to that.  Finally, in a metroidvania-style twist you’ll unlock some traversal upgrades along the way, and these are generally fun and easy to use, though I did find that much like in Blasphemous 2 they diminished the more gritty, realistic dark fantasy Ska Studios was attempting to create.  Still, Salt & Sanctuary is, for the most part, Dark Souls on a 2d-plane, successfully transposing it’s dark setting and labyrinthian level design, but failing to capture its signature combat and gripping enemy encounters.

[h1][b]「希望さえも錆びつき、やがて塩へと還る」[/b][/h1]  [h1]ストーリー[/h1]  長きにわたる戦争を終わらせる政略結婚のため、隣国へ向かう王女を護送していた船団。  しかし、海から現れた謎の巨大生物によって船は破壊され、乗組員たちは海へと投げ出される。 主人公が目を覚ましたのは、地図にも載っていない、霧と死臭が立ち込める絶海の孤島。そこは、死してなお蘇り、永遠に戦い続けることを強いられる「塩」に呪われた地だった。  [h1]概要[/h1]  『Dark Souls』シリーズへの多大なるリスペクトと、探索型2Dアクション（メトロイドヴァニア）を融合させた、2Dソウルライクの始祖にして最高傑作。  プレイヤーは600以上の武器、防具、魔法を駆使し、入り組んだダンジョンを探索する。経験値であり通貨でもある「塩（Salt）」を集め、聖域（サンクチュアリ）でレベルアップを行うシステムや、死ねば塩をその場に落とす緊張感など、本家のヒリつく難易度を見事に2Dへと落とし込んでいる。  [h1]感想[/h1]  [b]模倣を超え、独自の「神話」を築き上げたダークファンタジーの傑作。[/b]  本作を単なる「2D版ダークソウル」と呼ぶのは簡単だが、それだけでは語り尽くせない魅力がある。特筆すべきは、タイトルにもある通り「塩」を中心とした徹底的な世界観の構築だ。敵も、通貨も、神々さえも、すべてが塩と海に結びついている。手書きのアートスタイルが醸し出す、湿り気を帯びた陰鬱な空気感は圧倒的で、プレイしているだけで肌に潮風がベトつくような錯覚さえ覚える。  また、独自の「信仰（Creed）」システムにより、崇める神によって回復アイテムやエンチャント（付与効果）が変化する点も、ロールプレイの深みを増しており、この陰惨な世界に深く浸らせてくれる。  [h1]評価[/h1]  【GOOD】  ・本家に勝るとも劣らない、完成度の高いダークファンタジーの空気感。  ・「塩」や「信仰」をテーマにした独自性の強い世界観と、考察しがいのある深い設定。  ・広大なマップと自由度の高い育成要素による、探索の楽しさ。  【BAD】  ・2Dアクションの宿命として、戦闘が平面的になりがちで、中盤以降は立ち回りがマンネリ化しやすい。  ・ダメージを受けた際の「吹っ飛び」アニメーションが大きく、硬直も長いため、そのまま落下死したりハメ殺されたりといった理不尽なストレス要因になりやすい。

Прародитель 2D-соулс-метроидваний, которого я избегала из-за странного визуального стиля, кажущегося несколько карикатурным. Интересно, что вышел он за год до Hollow Knight.  Главный герой попадает в кораблекрушение и очухивается на странном острове, полном враждебных существ. Редко встречаемые дружелюбные персонажи попали на остров точно так же, но не совсем понятно, на одном корабле они все плыли или всё-таки на разных, в разное время. Остров полон разнообразных биомов, исследовать его, открывая короткие пути обратно и новые способности, интересно.  RPG составляющая неплоха. В начале предлагается выбор из нескольких классов, два из них начинают игру с первым уровнем и без способностей, остальные — на втором или третьем уровне с одной или двумя способностями. Отличаются классы стартовой экипировкой. Дерево навыков огромно и позволяет изучать боевую магию, защитную магию (молитвы), владение разными видами оружия и возможность носить броню, а также улучшать характеристики (силу, ловкость, выносливость и другие). Я обычно предпочитаю с минимумом страданий бегать с двуручником и лёгкой бронёй, так же играла и здесь.  К сожалению, игра офигеть как минималистична; кроме названий стартовых классов и значков экипировки, игрок при старте не располагает никакой информацией. Ещё можно выбрать начальный предмет — статую, кольцо и т.д.; и тут тоже не понять, что они делают, пока не начнёшь игру и не зайдёшь в инвентарь, чтобы почитать.  Боевая система заключается в применении заклинаний, парирований, блокирований и перекатов. Парирование тут весьма щадящее, но игру можно пройти практически без него — боссы всё равно не парируются, лишь один рядовой враг попался очень жирный, которого пришлось трижды запарировать, чтобы убить. Блокировать можно оружием или щитом, и опять же, вся игра легко проходится без него. Конечно, всё завязано на полоске выносливости, которую расходуют удары, блоки, перекаты, а магия так и вовсе её кушает и уменьшает до следующего сохранения.  Боссы не сказать что разнообразны, большинство из них побеждается алгоритмом «ударить разок, поуворачиваться, ударить ещё разок». Очень сложный босс был дракон, у которого все атаки, оказалось, бьют только по земле, и можно подпрыгивать и из воздуха безопасно бить его по лицу. Ещё интересный босс — дерево из мужиков, но во время боя больше происходит поединок с самим собой, слишком легко прыгнуть или перекатиться за пределы арены и умереть от падения; все 10 раз, что я умирала, происходили именно из-за моей криворукости. В конце игры есть боссы с магией, которая убивает с одного попадания, что обидно, для победы нужно понять систему, по которой летят снаряды и как от них увернуться. Броня имеет разные параметры устойчивости к разным стихиям, нужно понять, чем бьют враги на локации и боссы, и одеваться соответствующе.  В игре две валюты — соль и золото. Соль используется для прокачки, золото — для торговли. Игра во второй половине становится ужасно не сбалансирована, потому что золото тратить особо не на что, кроме: ящиков соли, чемоданов соли, мешков соли, щепоток соли. В результате просто сливаешь золото в соль и качаешься. К финальному боссу у меня был 90-й уровень, и когда у него не оказалось даже второй фазы, единственное, что я могла сказать — это «и что, всё?». Уровень боссов не скейлится до уровня игрока, так что при перекаче многие улетают с первой же попытки.  При смерти всю соль тырит убивший игрока враг (или же она материализуется в летучей мыши, если игрок убивает себя сам платформингом — мой случай). Если соль стырил босс, то достаточно вернуть её, сняв боссу около четверти здоровья. Если не успеть вернуть соль и снова умереть, то она исчезает навсегда. [url=https://images.steamusercontent.com/ugc/18238690266024529316/3D541B4009C72573D3AA6323989EAFFF4084A484/]Вот это было немного больно.[/url]  Также при смерти игрока воскрешает таинственный целитель, который тырит часть золота. В общем, лучший вариант — это постоянно сливать соль и деньги в повышение уровня и торговлю и не копить их, чтобы не было жалко.  Есть особый предмет, позволяющий убрать любой навык и вернуть очко прокачки. Все навыки соединены в цепочки, однако можно убирать навыки, даже разрывая цепочку — все последующие остаются открытыми. Данных предметов в игре достаточно, чтобы исправить, но не полностью изменить билд, поэтому если вы начали играть тяжёлым рыцарем, а потом вдруг решили стать чистым магом — извините, так не получится.  Точки сохранения представляют собой святилища разных верований, и забавно, что игрок в процессе прохождения может менять веру в каждом новом святилище. У святилищ свои полезности, например, по-разному работают хилки — у одной религии это булка, которая моментально восстанавливает здоровье, но очень долго кушается; у другой это пузырёк с чем-то непонятным, который тоже быстро поднимает здоровье, но может вызвать отравление при применении нескольких штук за короткий промежуток. При сохранении восстанавливаются все хилки и некоторые расходники (зависит от веры). Святилища можно прокачивать — нанимать торговцев, ремесленников, а также создателей порталов к другим ранее посещённым святилищам. Кузнецы могут заточить оружие до 7 раз и улучшить броню, но требуют за это редкие ресурсы, так что много вещей обновить не выйдет.  Из не очень приятного — я казуал и люблю, чтобы боссы находились ровно в двух метрах от точки сохранения; здесь же до них приходится немного бежать, часто с платформингом и через врагов.  В целом это интересная, мрачная игра, своего рода классика, с которой было занятно ознакомиться. Разработчики внезапно после, кажется, восьми лет выхода игры ещё и выпустили патч для баланса, в начале выбирается, классическую или «улучшенную» (мой вариант) боёвку хочет игрок.

🍚 First and foremost, this is NOT a metroidvania, as it literally lacks the key elements of the genre. It's a 2D Souls game, nothing more. The visual is good and it's full of what people love about Souls-likes, but if you're not a fan, there's nothing to do here. While it doesn't impose insane difficulty barrier and offers a ton of interesting content to explore, try, collect, and so on, it's quite repetitive. It's monotonous wandering through similar platforming labyrinths (w/o a map, of course), equip has stats, but they're not noticeable, the lore is another vague interpretation of something, but perhaps it'll make more sense later on, all battles are: block or dodge, poke, repeat. And I understand that scary and dangerous enemies add satisfaction to the subsequent imaginary feeling of overcoming an obstacle, but when exploring locations is simply a waste of time, even without trial and error, why bother? This isn't a problem with the game, but with the Souls genre, whose frustrating elements are sometimes exploited more than necessary.

Salt and Sanctuary es, sinceramente, mi Souls-like favorito. Lo ha sido desde que lo probé por primera vez en PlayStation Vita. Para mi desgracia, no he tenido la oportunidad de jugar títulos como Blasphemous o Hollow Knight, pero eso no cambia mi punto: este juego me parece excepcional. Considero que uno de sus mayores aciertos es la enorme variedad que ofrece en todos los aspectos: clases, armas, estrategias y formas de desenvolverte en el mapa. Puedes abordar el juego de muchas maneras distintas, tanto al enfrentarte a los enemigos comunes como a los jefes, e incluso en la forma en que manejas el grindeo. Narrativamente también es sobresaliente. Tiene un lore muy sólido detrás, que se siente presente a lo largo de toda la experiencia. Además, el sistema de atajos está magníficamente diseñado: poco a poco te das cuenta de que todo el mapa está interconectado, algo que no siempre notas de inmediato, pero que hace que la exploración sea mucho más satisfactoria y completa. Algunos jefes pueden llegar a sentirse un poco injustos al principio, pero es más una cuestión de aprender sus patrones y “agarrarles la maña”. Una vez que los entiendes, el reto se vuelve muy disfrutable. Para mí, este juego es claramente un 10 de 10. Lo recomiendo ampliamente, sobre todo si eres fan de los metroidvania, la fantasía oscura, el género de espada y brujería o de Dark Souls. Incluso tiene referencias muy interesantes a obras como Berserk. Francamente, me encanta. Este juego es una maravilla.

Of all the Soulslikes I've played, which are quite a few, this is probably the one that resembles Dark Souls 1 the most, only its in 2D. Thats not a bad thing in my book given thats one of my favorite games of all time. Most of its mechanics, systems and stats are also found here. Only a few things like the skill tree and stone merchants at the bonfires differs. So as someone who loves this genre I enjoyed this one, and the artstyle, world and atmosphere is amazing. Very grim and dark, so dark that in fact that torches or other light sources are a must in some places, which builds the tension, not being able to use shield/offhand and not knowing what lurks in the dark etc. Great stuff. It has decent bosses too, there are alot of them with some ofc being better than others.  The only real criticism I have is that in its attempt to take after its inspiration, they decided to not include any maps whatsoever, and while that works well in Dark Souls, in 2D platformers it doesnt I think. All other 2D "metroidvania soulslikes" like Blasphemous, Hollow Knight, Mandragora, The Last Faith etc. all have in-game maps. The amount of times you'll get lost not knowing where you are or where to go, or have to take leaps of faith jumping off ledges not able to see what is below, only to fall to your death, especially late-game its brutal with several of these ledges below you cant see, in a row. Its a trial and error of falling to death each time to try remember where each ledge is, sometimes with long runback to not lose your souls (salt), its just too much imo and not very fun. Fall damage caused about 80% of all deaths, go figure.  Thankfully there are maps on wikis online, but i'd much rather not have to resort to that. But there's no way i'd finish this game without them, and I doubt there are honestly that many players that completed this game completely blind first playthrough (If anyone reading this did complete this game without checking anything online, then you deserve a reward, lol)  In short, in 3D souls games I like the 'no maps' design, in 2D ones I'd def prefer maps, and to my knowledge all of them have it except this one, so yeah.. If you can get past the 'no maps' and the many 'falling to your death' stuff thats bound to happen, then its a great game overall and highly recommend it if you like these kind of games! Just remember there are maps on the wikis! xD

[b]Salt and Sanctuary[/b] — гра, з якою у мене склалися довгі і відверто непрості стосунки. Вона одночасно подобається і постійно нагадує, що ти тут ніхто, і це тобі доведеться відчути на власному досвіді. Я закидав її тричі протягом багатьох років, але, звісно, повернувся, бо хто відмовиться від ще однієї порції страждань і нервових зривів? З четвертої спроби нарешті дістався фіналу і, на власне здивування, пройшов гру двічі поспіль, остаточно закривши цей давній гештальт і трохи похваливши себе за витривалість.  Перші спроби були суцільним болем і повним непорозумінням. Я блукав світом, як сліпий котик, постійно задаючись питанням «А куди мені взагалі йти?» і отримував відповідь у вигляді миттєвої смерті від звичайного ворога. Боси нагадували, що моє місце — біля вівтаря, а не на полі бою. Справжнє задоволення прийшло під час останнього проходження, коли я нарешті навчився виживати, зрозумів, як стартувати без саморуйнування, яку релігію обрати і що будь-яка невірна відповідь у діалогах з жителями світу може назавжди знищити їхню сюжетну лінію, залишивши мене в повному здивуванні. Відсутність мапи робила світ великим і заплутаним лабіринтом, а мої дії карали без попереджень, іноді просто за компанію.  У фінальному проходженні я вирішив витиснути з гри максимум. Зібрав найкращий комплект броні і створив один із найсильніших видів зброї для свого класу, хоч трохи не розрахував із вагою обладунків й відчував себе ходячим рюкзаком. Вийшла довга, важка і весело мучительна пригода, після якої можна було або впасти на підлогу від радості, або просто почати сміятися над усім цим хаосом. Якщо ви любите соулслайки з елементами метроїдванії і готові страждати, щоб потім отримати кайф від виживання, гра вас не розчарує. Грати чи ні — вирішуйте самі, а моя багаторічна епопея з [b]Salt and Sanctuary[/b] нарешті завершена, хоча душа вже підозріло сумує за новими муками.

Salt and Sanctuary é uma verdadeira joia para quem ama metroidvanias e soulslike. O jogo entrega uma atmosfera sombria e envolvente, com mapas interconectados que recompensam cada passo da exploração. O combate é desafiador e profundo, permitindo builds variadas — seja focado em força bruta, magia ou híbridos. As marcas (Brands) funcionam como chaves de progressão no melhor estilo Metroidvania, abrindo novas rotas e mantendo a sensação constante de descoberta. A trilha sonora e o visual desenhado à mão criam uma identidade única, que mistura melancolia e brutalidade de forma impecável. Cada chefe é memorável, exigindo estratégia e paciência, mas a sensação de vitória é sempre recompensadora. Mesmo após terminar, o New Game+ mantém o desafio vivo, incentivando novas tentativas e diferentes estilos de jogo. Para quem busca uma experiência intensa, desafiadora e cheia de segredos, Salt and Sanctuary é obrigatório.

This game is absolutely fantastic. It's been a WHILE since any game has had a hold on me like this. Salt and Sanctuary feels like a game where EVERYTHING it sets out to accomplish, it passes with flying colors.   The combat feels smooth and with things like being surrounded by smaller enemies or facing off bigger ones, it's very easy to be on the edge of your seat. The bosses are even BETTER. I love the designs, love the lore and love their move sets. There has NOT BEEN A SINGLE BOSS IN THIS GAME that felt samey. Even when they had similar ideas, they all held their own unique identity.  The Music is amazing. The developers ABSOLUTELY went with "quality over quantity". Even though the ost list is not incredibly extensive, it's completely memorable. The boss themes and "Sanctuary" live rent free in my head.  The game also sits on that sweet spot of "frustration" at times where it's not enough to make me quit. Whenever a boss defeated me, or I made a mistake, or something happened I didn't expect, my mental response was always "alright, I'll get em THIS time!" The only exceptions were on one particular boss fight, which I still managed to beat pretty alright, and at 2 points where I got lost and didn't know exactly where I needed to go. Both of them were due to me missing something mega small, but it can happen.  My only other issue with this game, this is a minor one, is that after teaching you how to Parry, the game never informs you that not everything CAN or SHOULD be parried. Once I understood that dodging attacks is usually preferred, that's when the game really clicked for me.  I still would mega absolutely 10000%. recommend this game for anyone who would love a "souls like" to play.

So if you want a hybrid of DS 1 Blighttown and Celeste, here you go! Have fun.  Extremely challening and sadistic (and I am a DS/Sekiro/Nioh player). So bring a TON of frustration tolerance. I would say you have to be masochistic to play this game.  There is a lot of platforming in this game, often under time pressure while enemy mobs are pursuing you. You will die a LOT during your jumping exercises because the jumping is not the most precise in the world.  Normal enemies follow you into the boss room, so suddenly there might be a skeleton walking along while you are fighting the boss which itself is almost impossible to defeat.  The bosses, some are "easy", some are hardcore. The hard ones bombard you with pistol shots, homing missiles or minions so that the reaction time windows are extremely small. You have to pay attention to 5 things at once.   Often, enemies are not visible because they look too much like the background.  You will have to Google your way through the game because maps are interconnected via countless shortcuts and you will lose overview soon.  The game is quite long. There are a lot of maps and since the maps are very hard, it will take a lot of time to explore everything. For my taste, the game is a little too long.   The graphics and art design are very good, nothing to complain here. The overall atmosphere is very good.  The design and ideas for the bosses are very creative. But some of them are very tricky.  The good thing is, in the end you will level up so much that you can get a much better weapon which makes your life a LOT easier.  Overall, it was a bit boring.  I would say 6/10.

Top-tier Soulslike Metroidvania that masterfully blends challenging, methodical combat with deep exploration, a vast interconnected world, stunning hand-drawn art, and satisfying progression through its huge skill tree, making it a highly recommended and immersive experience despite minor critiques about its darkness and lack of an in-game map which adds an extra layer of challenge and boasts high rewards for exploration. It's addictive risk/reward loop, excellent atmosphere, quirky sound design, and significant content, solidifying its status as a benchmark for its genre.   If therir goal was to make 2D Dark Souls, they certainly succeeded with Salt and Sanctuary, a punishing, side-scrolling action game where you create a hero and make your way through a challenging world filled with darkness, overbearing enemies, and lots of death. While my time with Salt and Sanctuary reminded me of some Soulsian frustrations I’m a huge fan of, it also shined a new light on the poise and elegance of the style that requires a mastery of mechanics to survive and progress.  This world is brought to life in a distinctive hand-drawn style that is almost equally brutal and goofy, calling to mind the developer’s past work on more arcade-like beat-’em-ups like Charlie Murder. The tone is pitch black, but little things like the cartoony look of character faces bring tiny bits of levity to the forefront. That weird contrast helps make the overall brutality a little duller but overall more palpable. And brutal this game is, as the side-scrolling battles require precision and deftness, accounting for the length your attack animations take while also keeping an eye on the enemy so you can time your parry or riposte and try not to be overwhelmed. It’s a constant challenge and the open, level-less design makes it easy to stumble upon an enemy that’s potentially out of your league.  The overall layout is reminiscent of a Metroidvania, but for better or worse, it has no map. Thankfully, the flow is straightforward enough for this to not be a game-breaking issue, but the lack of guidance made me a little more cautious of going off what seemed to be the main path. It is engrossing enough that getting lost is fun, but not so much when you get your head beat in by a difficult enemy and then you’re not sure where to go next. But none the less it is an excellent journey worth taking for sure!   That all comes back around to how, at its core, Salt and Sanctuary is 2D Dark Souls. I say that not as any indictment but more of a very adept comparison. Ska Studios smartly transformed the core concepts of a Souls game and turned it into a side-scrolling action game with an ample amount of Metroidvania influence. The result is tough as nails, but doable with perseverance and gumption. Salt and Sanctuary is not for the faint of heart, but it’s a journey worth suffering through for those who think they are worthy. Juegazo y encima muy bien de precio, le he acabado sacando todos los logros. Salt and Sanctuary es un souls-like metroidvania que creo que se diferencia de otros porque es más souls-like que metroidvania. Gracias a esto creo que ha sido el único juego del género que me ha enganchado tanto (he probado Hollow Knight, Blasphemous y Axiom Verge), este juego bebe mucho de Dark Souls 1 y casi lo podrías tratar como una versión 2D. La dificultad encuentro que es bastante buena, algunos jefes son desafiantes pero ninguno injusto, los controles son muy buenos y la exploración se siente recompensante sobre todo con los atajos (de ahí que me recuerda a Dark Souls 1). Lo único que no me ha gustado tanto del juego (que no afecta casi) es que el estilo artístico, pese a ser muy bonito y estilo hand-drawn, a veces no pega muy bien con los enemigos y pueden ser algo difíciles de ver, y también que considero que hay algunas áreas del juego o jefes donde el daño ambiental es casi más mortífero que otra cosa haciendo algún que otro runback algo frustrante (la mayoría de jefes tienen un santuario cerca así que son pocos casos). En resumidas cuentas si te gustan los juegos de tipo souls este juego seguramente te guste, pero ten eso muy en mente porque si buscas un metroidvania puro y duro, este quizá no sea adecuado por el estilo de juego basado en stamina/rodar y su dificultad.

之前一直想玩来着的，听说是2D版黑魂，但是都没蹲到过打折（虽然原价也不是很贵），趁着冬促入了。整体流程体验下来确实很有黑魂的味道，可以说是充满黑魂味的类银河战士恶魔城了，地图的设计还是满优秀的，战斗上因为魂系一直玩的都是力敏战士，拿到大剑发现大剑无脑强，转化剪刀剑后配合黄金酒一刀一个大动脉，确实可以改名为“大剑与黄金酒”哈哈哈。剧情上有着魂系的晦涩传统，加上本身流程并不长，也没怎么懂背景故事，印象比较深的NPC也就小偷女和盾枪哥。宗教教派的设定蛮有意思，不同教派有不同的信仰和道具，流派加点也还算丰富，一周目的话剪刀剑黄金酒乱砍就完事了，还可以附魔增伤，BOSS基本没有什么难度（一周目，听说高周目需要配合各种增伤法术祷告）。个人感觉有些小怪比BOSS还烦，制作组挺喜欢弄些“阴怪”的，灰烬女啊，教堂的狗，森林直接扑脸的面团怪直接给你中毒等等，关键是这游戏的画面背景本就偏暗灰色系，有一层朦胧的薄雾覆盖着，让这些怪更加难对付了。但是鉴于本体流程的时长，没有让我觉得有很大的“恶意”。 总体来说，这是一款很对魂游和类银魔爱好者玩家胃口的游戏，游戏小而精，流派的可玩性还是有的，推荐游玩
